task_desc: Fix Brain3D tooltip ghost and education panel closing when interacting with 3D brain -- Two event handler bugs that break the education panel + Brain3D dual-panel workflow: (1) brain3d.js:176 registers only a mousemove handler on the renderer canvas (Brain3D._onMouseMove) with no mouseleave or mouseout handler; when the user hovers a brain region (tooltip appears with region name, description, neuron activations) then moves the mouse off the canvas -- to the toolbar, education panel, or outside the browser window -- the tooltip remains visible at its last position with stale content because no event fires to hide it; the tooltip (z-index:45, position:fixed, pointer-events:none) floats as a ghost element until the user moves back over the canvas and misses a region; fix by adding a mouseleave listener on Brain3D._renderer.domElement in show() (paired with removal in hide()) that sets Brain3D._tooltipEl.style.display = 'none'. (2) The document click listener at main.js:366-375 that closes the education panel on outside clicks does not exclude the brain3d-overlay; any click or mousedown-mouseup on the 3D brain canvas (for OrbitControls rotation, zoom, or simple clicks) propagates to document and closes the education panel because the canvas is outside the education-panel element; this makes it impossible to rotate/pan the 3D brain while the education panel is open, which is the intended workflow for the region highlight feature (user reads about a region in the education panel, clicks the region name to highlight it in 3D, then rotates the brain to view it); fix by adding brain3d-overlay to the exclusion check in the document click handler alongside the education-panel and learnBtn checks. Files: js/brain3d.js, js/main.js.
